active_paradigms.idea: Generalized power-law logistic ODE: dc/dt = (c0 + c1*x1) * x2^c2 * (1 - x2)
active_paradigms.description: Replace the shifted linear nucleation term (c2 + x2) with a power-law term x2^c2. The exponent c2 is a fitted constant that controls the shape of the sigmoid: c2<1 gives faster early growth matching seeded aggregation (no lag phase), c2=1 gives standard logistic, c2>1 gives slower early growth matching unseeded aggregation with nucleation lag. This is directly motivated by the Richards/generalized logistic equation and the power-law nucleation kinetics in Knowles et al. amyloid models. The expression is always non-negative for x2>=0 (unlike the current model which can go negative for small x2 with c2<0), avoiding spurious decreasing trajectories. The rate factor (c0 + c1*x1) preserves the x1-dependent rate scaling that works well. This targets the hard cases (Abeta40 seeded nmse=0.1316) where the current model's negative nucleation offset causes poor fit.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce nmse_val on seeded datasets (Abeta40 seeded 0.1316, Abeta42 seeded 0.0548) by allowing flexible sigmoid shape via power-law exponent; maintain good fit on unseeded datasets
active_paradigms.cautions: x2^c2 requires x2>0; since x2 is initialized from observed[0] (first data point), it should be positive in practice. Use initial_values=[1.0, 0.5, 1.0] to start at standard logistic. Watch for c2->0 degeneracy. Complexity ~18 nodes.

active_paradigms.idea: Asymmetric logistic with quadratic x2 correction: dc/dt = (c0 + c1*x1) * x2 * (1 - x2) * (1 + c2*x2)
active_paradigms.description: Add a cubic correction term (1 + c2*x2) to the standard logistic that breaks left-right symmetry of the sigmoid. When c2>0, growth is faster in the upper half (matching fast-completing seeded reactions); when c2<0, growth is faster in the lower half (matching nucleation-dominated unseeded reactions). This is the Tsoularis-Wallace generalization of logistic growth. The model is always well-defined (no singularities, no non-real values), keeps x2=0 as a fixed point (correct for unseeded starting from zero), and adds only one extra constant over a 2-constant logistic. The structure x2*(1-x2)*(1+c2*x2) is a cubic polynomial in x2 that can represent diverse sigmoid shapes. Rate factor (c0+c1*x1) preserved from current best.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Improve fit on asymmetric sigmoidal datasets (seeded Abeta40/42) while keeping good performance on symmetric cases; same 3 constants as current model
active_paradigms.cautions: For large c2 and x2 near 1, the (1+c2*x2) term could become negative, causing unphysical behavior. Keep initial_values=[1.0, 0.5, 0.0] to start from symmetric logistic. The cubic term may occasionally overshoot plateau.

active_paradigms.idea: Two-pathway model with additive nucleation and elongation: dc/dt = (c0 + c1*x1) * (x2 + c2*x2^2) * (1 - x2)
active_paradigms.description: Model two parallel growth pathways: primary elongation (x2 term, linear in existing aggregates) and secondary nucleation (c2*x2^2 term, quadratic in aggregates, representing fibril-catalyzed nucleation). This is directly motivated by the Knowles master equation where secondary nucleation rate scales as M^2 (fibril mass squared). The combined factor (x2 + c2*x2^2) = x2*(1 + c2*x2) is always non-negative for x2>=0 and c2>=-1/x2_max. When c2>0, secondary nucleation accelerates growth in the middle of the curve (explaining faster-than-logistic seeded kinetics). When c2=0, reduces to standard logistic. This is physically distinct from the current model because it uses x2^2 rather than a constant offset, making nucleation concentration-dependent rather than spontaneous.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Capture secondary nucleation physics for seeded datasets while maintaining generality; the x2^2 term specifically targets the accelerated mid-curve growth seen in seeded Abeta40
active_paradigms.cautions: Factor out x2: dc/dt = (c0+c1*x1)*x2*(1+c2*x2)*(1-x2). This is equivalent to idea 2 with different parameterization. Ensure c2 initial value is 0 (starts at logistic). May have numerical issues if c2 becomes very large negative.
